summary: Code-quality sweep to satisfy local `melee-issues` diagnostics without changing GALE01 output. The diff mainly converts old/no-argument C definitions to strict prototypes, cleans a Zebes `StageCallbacks` initializer, removes or reorders small header/style issues, and normalizes formatting across fighter, game-mode, stage, item, and menu code. The PR body was empty, so intent is inferred from the title, code-quality label, diff, and bot report.
what_changed: Converted old or non-prototype function definitions to strict C prototypes, including `fn_800DBED4(gobj) Fighter_GObj* gobj;` to `void fn_800DBED4(Fighter_GObj* gobj)` and several `func()` definitions to `func(void)`.
Updated no-argument inline/static helpers such as `fn_801A7FB4_inline`, `fn_801A7FB4_inline2`, `isActiveTitle`, `grShrineRoute_8020A8A4_rand`, `ordering_func`, and `GetNumNameList` to use `(void)`.
In `gm_1832.c`, collapsed `fn_8018846C_noInline` from a separate declaration plus old-style definition into `static int fn_8018846C_noInline(void)`.

In `grzebes.c`, moved early Zebes lifecycle definitions below the stage data block and rewrote `grZe_callbacks` entries to use direct callback names and scalar flags instead of casts and `{ 0 }` union-style initializers.
Removed `#include "mp/mplib.h"` from `ftCo_Attack100.c`, reordered `itwhispyapple.h` in `grgreens.c`, and removed an extra blank line in `grmaterial.h`.
Normalized formatting, cast spacing, and line wrapping in `ftdynamics.c`, `grgreens.c`, `grbigblueroute.c`, and `ftPp_SpecialS.c` without evidence of behavior changes.
decomp-dev bot reported `No changes` for GALE01 after the PR.
smart_moves: Fixed strict-prototype-style diagnostics at the source level instead of suppressing warnings, preserving the project’s libclang-based `melee-issues` signal.
Kept bodies and control flow unchanged while changing declarations/prototypes, which is a low-risk way to make lint fixes codegen-neutral.
Used existing typed declarations for stage callbacks in `grzebes.c` rather than keeping redundant casts around `StageCallbacks` entries.
Made the `fn_8018846C_noInline` helper local with `static` while also giving it a strict `(void)` prototype, matching its apparent file-local wrapper role.
Relied on decomp.dev’s no-diff report to validate that style/prototype cleanup did not perturb the matched binary.
decomp_lessons: `void func()` is not a strict C prototype; use `void func(void)` for no-argument functions, including inline helpers and ordering/noinline helpers.
Old K&R-style parameter definitions can usually be converted to modern prototypes with the same typed parameters without changing generated code.
Callback tables should prefer direct, type-compatible function names over casts when headers or typedefs already describe the right signature.
Union/scalar flag fields in stage callback tables can be initialized with scalar constants such as `0` or `0xC0000000` when that matches the struct definition.
Lint-only PRs should still be checked with decomp.dev or equivalent matching reports; here the bot’s `No changes` comment is the main verification evidence.
When a PR has no body or human comments, document that intent is inferred from title and diff rather than reviewer discussion.
naming_conventions: No broad renaming was performed; address-based decomp names such as `fn_80171DC4`, `grZebes_801D84A4`, and `grGreens_80213C10` were preserved.
Helper suffixes such as `_inline`, `_inline2`, and `_noInline` were preserved while fixing their prototypes.
Use `(void)` for no-argument C functions and inline helpers rather than empty parameter lists.
Stage callback arrays retain stage-specific prefixes such as `grZe_callbacks` and function prefixes such as `grZebes_`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Prototype-only changes such as `func()` to `func(void)` and K&R-to-prototype conversion are useful lint fixes because they need not alter codegen.
Preserving `PAD_STACK(4)` and the existing inline wrapper call in `fn_800DBED4` avoided disturbing a matched stack/layout tactic while cleaning the declaration.
For `StageCallbacks`, keep callback order and flag constants unchanged when removing casts or changing initializer syntax; table layout is the matching-sensitive part.
Formatting changes to casts, wrapping, and braces should be paired with a no-diff check because they are intended to be source hygiene only.
The decomp.dev `No changes` report is evidence that this PR’s edits were binary-neutral for GALE01.
review_feedback: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs fixing `melee-issues` or `-Wstrict-prototypes` diagnostics
Search for remaining `void func()` no-argument definitions in src/melee
Search for old K&R-style function definitions in matched fighter code
Find StageCallbacks initializers still using casts or `{ 0 }` flag initializers
Compare other no-diff code-quality PRs with decomp-dev `No changes` reports
